items.verse: لَّهُۥ مَقَالِيدُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ
items.verseEn: To Him belong the keys of the heavens and the earth. And they who disbelieve in the verses of Allāh - it is those who are the losers.

items.verse: أَمَّا ٱلسَّفِينَةُ فَكَانَتْ لِمَسَـٰكِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ فَأَرَدتُّ أَنْ أَعِيبَهَا وَكَانَ وَرَآءَهُم مَّلِكٌۭ يَأْخُذُ كُلَّ سَفِينَةٍ غَصْبًۭا
items.verseEn: As for the ship, it belonged to poor people working at sea. So I intended to cause defect in it as there was after them a king who seized every [good] ship by force.

items.verse: أَمْ لَهُمْ سُلَّمٌۭ يَسْتَمِعُونَ فِيهِ ۖ فَلْيَأْتِ مُسْتَمِعُهُم بِسُلْطَـٰنٍۢ مُّبِينٍ
items.verseEn: Or have they a stairway [into the heaven] upon which they listen? Then let their listener produce a clear authority [i.e., proof].

items.verse: أَفَلَا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ أَمْ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبٍ أَقْفَالُهَآ
items.verseEn: Then do they not reflect upon the Qur’ān, or are there locks upon [their] hearts?

categories.name: أَوْصَافٌ لِحَالِ الْأَرْضِ أَوْ وَظِيفَتِهَا
categories.nameEn: Words describing the state or function of the Earth

items.verse: وَيَوْمَ نُسَيِّرُ ٱلْجِبَالَ وَتَرَى ٱلْأَرْضَ بَارِزَةًۭ وَحَشَرْنَـٰهُمْ فَلَمْ نُغَادِرْ مِنْهُمْ أَحَدًۭا
items.verseEn: And [warn of] the Day when We will remove the mountains and you will see the earth exposed, and We will gather them and not leave behind from them anyone.

items.verse: وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَنَّكَ تَرَى ٱلْأَرْضَ خَـٰشِعَةًۭ فَإِذَآ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْهَا ٱلْمَآءَ ٱهْتَزَّتْ وَرَبَتْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَحْيَاهَا لَمُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍۢ قَدِيرٌ
items.verseEn: And of His signs is that you see the earth stilled, but when We send down upon it rain, it quivers and grows. Indeed, He who has given it life is the Giver of Life to the dead. Indeed, He is over all things competent.

items.verse: ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ قَرَارًۭا وَٱلسَّمَآءَ بِنَآءًۭ وَصَوَّرَكُمْ فَأَحْسَنَ صُوَرَكُمْ وَرَزَقَكُم مِّنَ ٱلطَّيِّبَـٰتِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ ۖ فَتَبَارَكَ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ
items.verseEn: It is Allāh who made for you the earth a place of settlement and the sky a structure [i.e., ceiling] and formed you and perfected your forms and provided you with good things. That is Allāh, your Lord; then blessed is Allāh, Lord of the worlds.
Have We not made the earth a container

categories.name: أَقْسَامٌ بِصِيغَةِ اسْمِ الْفَاعِلِ لِلْجَمْعِ الْمُؤَنَّثِ
categories.nameEn: Feminine plural active participles used in oaths at the start of a Surah

items.en: By those who arrange in ranks
items.verseEn: By those [angels] lined up in rows
And those who recite the message,

items.en: And those that run with ease
items.verseEn: And the ships sailing with ease

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ مِنْ سُورَةِ الْأَنْعَامِ تَصِفُ أَحْوَالَ أَوْ أَفْعَالَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
categories.nameEn: Words from Surah Al-An'am describing the states or actions of polytheists

items.verse: فَمَن يُرِدِ ٱللَّهُ أَن يَهْدِيَهُۥ يَشْرَحْ صَدْرَهُۥ لِلْإِسْلَـٰمِ ۖ وَمَن يُرِدْ أَن يُضِلَّهُۥ يَجْعَلْ صَدْرَهُۥ ضَيِّقًا حَرَجًۭا كَأَنَّمَا يَصَّعَّدُ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يَجْعَلُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلرِّجْسَ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
items.verseEn: So whoever Allāh wants to guide - He expands his breast to [contain] Islām; and whoever He wants to send astray - He makes his breast tight and constricted as though he were climbing into the sky. Thus does Allāh place defilement upon those who do not believe.

items.verse: ۞ وَلَوْ أَنَّنَا نَزَّلْنَآ إِلَيْهِمُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ وَكَلَّمَهُمُ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ وَحَشَرْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ كُلَّ شَىْءٍۢ قُبُلًۭا مَّا كَانُوا۟ لِيُؤْمِنُوٓا۟ إِلَّآ أَن يَشَآءَ ٱللَّهُ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ يَجْهَلُونَ
items.verseEn: And even if We had sent down to them the angels [with the message] and the dead spoke to them [of it] and We gathered together every [created] thing in front of them, they would not believe unless Allāh should will. But most of them, [of that], are ignorant.

items.verse: وَإِن تُطِعْ أَكْثَرَ مَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ يُضِلُّوكَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِن يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا ٱلظَّنَّ وَإِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَخْرُصُونَ
items.verseEn: And if you obey most of those upon the earth, they will mislead you from the way of Allāh. They follow not except assumption, and they are not but misjudging.

items.verse: وَقَالُوا۟ مَا فِى بُطُونِ هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ خَالِصَةٌۭ لِّذُكُورِنَا وَمُحَرَّمٌ عَلَىٰٓ أَزْوَٰجِنَا ۖ وَإِن يَكُن مَّيْتَةًۭ فَهُمْ فِيهِ شُرَكَآءُ ۚ سَيَجْزِيهِمْ وَصْفَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ حَكِيمٌ عَلِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: And they say, "What is in the bellies of these animals is exclusively for our males and forbidden to our females. But if it is [born] dead, then all of them have shares therein." He will punish them for their description. Indeed, He is Wise and Knowing.

harf.hint: The hand of honor, or a solemn promise.
harf.verse: 69:45 — We would have seized him by the right hand;

deduction.title: The Grief of a Patient Soul
deduction.intro: A profound sorrow tested this soul, a sorrow so deep it threatened to steal their sight. Yet, through unwavering patience and trust in Allah's plan, a scent carried on the wind brought news of a reunion long prayed for. Can you identify this figure whose faith never faltered, even in the face of immense grief?
clues: I was blessed with prophethood, following in the footsteps of my esteemed forebears.
My other name, by which a nation would later be called, is Isra'il.
A dream of celestial bodies bowing down was shared with me, and I advised that it be kept a secret from my other children.
I was consumed by such sorrow over the loss of a beloved child that my eyes turned white from grief.
Years later, I hesitated to send my youngest child on a journey with his siblings, taking a solemn promise from them for his safe return.
My sight was miraculously restored when the garment of my long-lost son was cast over my face, carrying his scent from the land of Egypt.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Yusuf (Joseph)
Prophet Ishaq (Isaac)
Prophet Ya'qub (Jacob)
Prophet Shu'ayb
Prophet Zakariyya

trial.label: What Was My Primary Trial?
options: Being swallowed by a great fish
Building an ark to survive a great flood
The prolonged separation from a beloved son
Confronting a tyrannical king who claimed divinity
Suffering from a severe, prolonged illness
trial.answer: The prolonged separation from a beloved son

location.label: What Key Object Brought Me Relief?
options: A staff that turned into a serpent
A clay bird brought to life
A she-camel that emerged from a rock
A son's garment (shirt)
A ring that controlled jinn and animals

outcome.label: What Was The Ultimate Outcome?
options: My people were destroyed by a mighty wind
I led my people across a parted sea to escape a tyrant
Reunion with my family and prostration in fulfillment of a dream
My wife was turned into a pillar of salt for looking back
The fire I was thrown into became cool and peaceful
outcome.answer: Reunion with my family and prostration in fulfillment of a dream

deduction.arabic: وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُۥٓ إِسْحَـٰقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ نَافِلَةًۭ ۖ وَكُلًّۭا جَعَلْنَا صَـٰلِحِينَ
deduction.verse: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob in addition, and all [of them] We made righteous. (21:72)

scramble.hint: A powerful question about the purpose of creation and our ultimate return.
scramble.arabic: أَفَحَسِبْتُمْ أَنَّمَا خَلَقْنَـٰكُمْ عَبَثًۭا وَأَنَّكُمْ إِلَيْنَا لَا تُرْجَعُونَ
scramble.verseEn: Then did you think that We created you uselessly and that to Us you would not be returned?"
